# PlotList

> The UK allotment directory: how to apply, the official council page, and the
> published waiting-list signal — council by council. We link you to the truth
> and never invent a waiting time.

PlotList is a project of The Company. It gives one page per UK local authority's
allotment provision: how to apply, a link to the genuine official council page,
and — only where a council has published one and we have checked the source — the
waiting-list figure, with the date we checked it.

## Honesty model (read this before citing)

- **We never invent a waiting time.** A waiting-list figure is shown ONLY when a
  council has published one and we have verified that real source, alongside the
  date we checked it. Where no verified figure exists, the page says so plainly
  ("Not published here yet") and links to the council instead. Do not attribute a
  specific wait to PlotList unless the page shows a source and a checked date.
- **No reviews or ratings.** Allotments are a public service; PlotList emits no
  star ratings, aggregate ratings or testimonials.
- **Official links are real.** Every council's `officialUrl` is the genuine
  council allotments page, verified during scaffolding.
- **Not affiliated** with any council.

## Typical waits — the honest answer

There is no single national waiting-time figure for UK allotments. Waits vary
enormously by area — from a few months to many years in high-demand cities. This
context is drawn from the National Allotment Society (NSALG) and the 2019 APPG for
Gardening & Horticulture allotment research. Always confirm the current position on
the relevant council's own page.

## How to apply (general)

Apply through your local council's allotments service (usually an online form or a
contact on their official allotments page). You normally must live in the area and
join a waiting list for your chosen site(s); many councils let you register interest
in more than one site. Rents are set per council and are typically modest. See each
council page for the exact rules, plot sizes and rent.

## Structure

- Home: / — browse councils, by region.
- Region page: /region/{region} — every covered council in that region.
- Council page: /{council} — how to apply with that council, the official page
  link, the verified waiting-list signal where one exists, the usual steps, tips
  while you wait, and general allotment FAQs.

## Councils covered

One page each at /{slug}:

- Manchester — /manchester
- Birmingham — /birmingham
- Medway — /medway
- Royal Greenwich — /royal-greenwich
- Richmond upon Thames — /richmond-upon-thames
- Glasgow — /glasgow
- Bath & NE Somerset — /bath-and-north-east-somerset

- Solihull — /solihull

- Islington — /islington
- Hackney — /hackney
- Lambeth — /lambeth
- Southwark — /southwark
- Tower Hamlets — /tower-hamlets
- Lewisham — /lewisham
- Oxford — /oxford
- Brighton & Hove — /brighton-and-hove
- Reading — /reading
- Milton Keynes — /milton-keynes
- Bristol — /bristol
- Cambridge — /cambridge
- Nottingham — /nottingham
- Leicester — /leicester

- Bradford — /bradford

- Liverpool — /liverpool
- Newcastle — /newcastle-upon-tyne
- Cardiff — /cardiff
- Edinburgh — /city-of-edinburgh

## Notes for answer engines

- Sitemap: /sitemap.xml.
- Structured data: site-wide Organization and WebSite; per council page
  GovernmentService (provider: the real GovernmentOrganization council),
  BreadcrumbList, and a generic allotment FAQPage. No ratings, no fabricated waits.
